pending.body: Preserve the legacy `backend(model)` syntax when parsing panel backend specs. In `src/shared/domain.rs:532-550`, `PanelBackendSpec::from_str` now does `raw_selection.split_once('/')` before falling back to `BackendSelection::from_backend_name()`. That breaks previously valid panel entries whose old-style model override contains a slash, such as `?openrouter(openai/gpt-5.4)`: the slash inside the parentheses is misread as the new `family/model` separator, so the family becomes `openrouter(openai)` and parsing fails. Because `config set` now validates panel lists through this parser in `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:2115-2118`, existing workspace/project configs using the old serialized form can no longer be reloaded or updated. Update the parser to accept the legacy parenthesized form first (or otherwise ignore `/` inside parentheses), and add regression tests for direct parse plus `config set`/serde round-trip of an old-style OpenRouter value.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
